source_jobs.title: Software Engineer - Drone Simulator
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As our GNC, ML, Embedded Software, and Validation teams scale, Harmattan AI requires dedicated internal software infrastructure to accelerate development and testing. Joining the Tooling team you will be responsible for developing a "state-of-the-art" drone simulator to support our advanced autonomous defence applications. This role offers high ownership and the opportunity to work at the intersection of software engineering, simulation, and AI robotics with direct mission impact.

 




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Simulator Development: Design, develop, and maintain flight-simulation-related tools and features.

 - Architectural Design: Define technical solutions, including architecture, APIs, and UIs, to respond to specific project needs.

 - Performance Optimisation: Analyze simulator performance across various aspects and implement necessary improvements.

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with GNC, ML, and Embedded teams to establish specifications for new features and ensure the tool meets user needs.

 - Documentation & Standards: Write clear documentation and tutorials, and eventually mentor junior developers to standardise best practices.
   
    
   
   


CANDIDATE REQUIREMENTS

 - Experience: Minimum of 3 years of professional experience in software engineering.

 - Technical Proficiency: Strong software skills in C++ and Python, with a good knowledge of Linux system internals.

 - Domain Expertise: Significant experience with drones or robotic systems, including practical understanding of embedded software (GNC, gimbal, video) and open-source autopilots like PX4 or Ardupilot.

 - Simulation & Graphics: Experience in development with game engines (Unreal Engine preferred) or contributing to 3D video game engines (physics or visual realism).

 - Educational Background: MSc or BSc in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Robotics, or a similar technical field; equivalent professional experience is equally valued.
   
    
   
   

 - Leadership & Mindset:
   
   - User Empathy: Ability to balance user feedback with technical vision to build effective R&D tools.
   
   - Problem Solving: Proven capacity to handle complex projects, from scoping and prioritising to delivering results under evolving requirements.
   
   - Commitment: 100% dedication to Harmattan AI’s mission and a "no-task-is-too-small" mentality in a fast-paced environment.



We look forward to hearing how you can help shape the future of autonomous defense systems at Harmattan AI.

source_jobs.title: France Business Developer Defense
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.






ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking an experienced and driven France Business Developer Defense to support our efforts in establishing and growing our presence in France's defense market. This role is critical to our expansion and success in a highly competitive sector. You will focus on building strategic relationships, driving revenue growth, and delivering product demonstrations to potential clients, showcasing our cutting-edge technologies.




RESPONSIBILITIES

Strategic Growth & Business Development

 - Identify and pursue new business opportunities with France's defense entities and key defense partners.

 - Develop and execute tailored business strategies to achieve measurable revenue and market share growth.

 - Maintain and manage a robust pipeline of opportunities, leveraging market analysis and competitor insights.

Sales & Client Engagement

 - Implement a comprehensive sales strategy to achieve growth objectives.

 - Develop and maintain relationships with key customers and partners in the region.

 - Conduct compelling product demonstrations to potential customers, effectively communicating the value and capabilities of Harmattan AI’s technologies.

 - Collaborate with the marketing team to develop and execute campaigns and programs to generate leads and drive sales.

 - Analyse sales data and market trends to identify new opportunities for growth.

 - Coordinate with R&D, product development, and supply chain teams to ensure smooth execution of sales initiatives.

 - Provide regular updates on sales performance and insights to the executive team.

Stakeholder & Government Relations

 - Cultivate relationships with operators, end-users, and high-ranking officials.

 - Represent Harmattan AI at high-profile meetings, negotiations, and industry events to enhance market positioning.




CANDIDATE REQUIREMENTS

 - Experience: Minimum of 3+ years in business development or sales in the defense sector.

 - Technical Expertise: Familiarity with advanced defense technologies.

 - Sales Acumen: Demonstrated ability to close complex deals and deliver effective product demonstrations.

 - Strategic Insight: Strong understanding of the defense sector procurement process and key market players.

 - Communication Skills: Exceptional verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to engage effectively with senior stakeholders.

 - Adaptability: A proactive and solution-oriented approach to navigating dynamic challenges and seizing opportunities.





We look forward to hearing how you can help shape the future of autonomous defense systems at Harmattan AI.

source_jobs.title: Propulsion Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As the propulsion requirements of Harmattan increase, we are expanding our team to accelerate the development of critical hardware and software tools. You will join a high-impact, two-person team focused on building state-of-the-art motor test benches and the software infrastructure used to visualise and share results across the entire organisation. This is a practice-oriented role that bridges the gap between Python software development and physical hardware testing.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - System Development: Build and maintain the hardware motor test bench to measure motor, propeller, and controller performance.

 - Software Engineering: Develop and improve internal Python-based tools for propulsion testbench operations and data visualisation.

 - Data Analysis: Create scripts to calculate motor thrust requirements and manage large datasets to extrapolate performance across different hardware configurations.

 - Testing & Reporting: Execute motor tests, upload results to our cloud platform, and author technical reports for internal stakeholders and manufacturers.

 - Collaborative Design: Participate in design meetings for new platforms (e.g., fixed-wing observers), providing expertise on propulsion solutions to meet range and autonomy specifications.

 - Tool Democratization: Ensure internal visualisation tools are robust and user-friendly for the wider Harmattan team.
   
   


CANDIDATE REQUIREMENTS

 - Educational Background: Engineering degree (Master’s or equivalent) in Mechatronics, Programming, or a related field; self-taught learners with exceptional portfolios will be considered.

 - Technical Proficiency: Strong Python programming skills, specifically for data analysis, plotting, and sensor integration.

 - Domain Expertise: Practical understanding of propulsion physics and experience with electric propulsion systems or UAV environments.

 - Complex Project Management: Ability to autonomously implement important features and move testing platforms to a state-of-the-art level within the first 90 days.

 - Proactive Problem Solving: A critical thinker who identifies edge cases and bottlenecks rather than just following instructions.

 - Communication & Influence: Ability to guide R&D teams on requirements and speak effectively with manufacturers regarding prototype results.

 - Commitment: 100% dedication to Harmattan AI’s mission, thriving in a rapid prototyping environment where speed and methodical testing are equally vital.



We look forward to hearing how you can help shape the future of autonomous defense systems at Harmattan AI.

source_jobs.title: Mechanical Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Mechanical Engineer on Harmattan AI’s Airframe team, you will design UAV platforms and subsystems in an autonomous, fast-paced environment. You will take ownership of projects from concept through to production, ensuring optimal performance, reliability, and manufacturability.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design and prototype UAV platforms and complex subsystems from scratch, ensuring the right balance between durability, efficiency, and performance.

 - Take full ownership of system-level constraints, including thermal management, mass budget (weight), COGS (cost), and environmental sealing (IP ratings).

 - Work closely with R&D teams (Electrical, Software, and Industrialisation) to ensure seamless mechanical integration of sensors, payloads, and electronics.

 - Conduct rigorous testing, including stress and failure analysis. Identify risks proactively and develop the necessary tools (test benches, calculation sheets, or scripts) to validate designs for operation in extreme environments.

 - Manage your own delivery packages and technical specifications with precision, consistently meeting or exceeding requirements under tight deadlines.

 - Oversee the manufacturing process by collaborating directly with suppliers and external manufacturers, with a focus on plastic injection moulding and precision machining, to ensure quality and schedule targets are achieved.

 - Maintain the “source of truth” through Confluence and professional 2D/3D documentation. Document all designs, test results, and production workflows to support knowledge sharing and continuous improvement.


CANDIDATE REQUIREMENTS

 - Master’s degree in Mechanical Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, or a related field.

 - Proven experience designing and prototyping mechanical systems, ideally within UAVs, robotics, or defence applications.

 - Proficiency in CAD software (e.g., SolidWorks); experience with structural and thermal analysis tools (FEA) is a strong advantage.

 - Strong understanding of manufacturing processes, including high-precision machining and plastic injection moulding.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to clearly document complex technical concepts and lead cross-functional discussions.

 - Full commitment to Harmattan AI’s mission, vision, and growth plans, with a willingness to go the extra mile in pursuit of operational excellence.



We look forward to hearing how you can help shape the future of autonomous defense systems at Harmattan AI.

source_jobs.title: Systems Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.



About the Role
As a System Engineer, you will own the architecture, system definition and implementation of one of our robotic systems — from concept to field deployment. You will work hand-in-hand with project managers and technical leads to translate mission needs into system-level designs, coordinate multidisciplinary engineering teams, and ensure overall system performance, robustness, and reliability.

Responsibilities

 - System Architecture: Transform system requirements into architectures solutions.

 - Technical Coordination: Lead cross-functional design across mechanical, electrical, software, GNC, and ML teams.

 - Trade-Off & Design Analysis: Conduct feasibility studies, trade-off analyses, and system-level simulations to guide technical choices.

 - Integration & Validation: Oversee system integration, prototyping, and validation, ensuring performance targets are achieved.

 - System Reviews: Drive design and architecture reviews, maintaining consistency between requirements, design, and testing.

 - Continuous Improvement: Implement processes to enhance requirements traceability, system documentation, and validation workflows.


Candidate Requirements

 - 10+ years of experience in system engineering within robotics, aerospace, or defense sectors.

 - MSc in Engineering (any field); PhD is a plus.

 - Proven experience leading full system lifecycles (concept → prototype → validation).

 - Strong background in system architecture, requirements engineering, and trade-off analysis.

 - Broad engineering culture with understanding of mechanical, electrical, and embedded systems.

 - Experience coordinating multi-disciplinary teams and managing technical interfaces.

 - Rigorous, structured, and mission-driven mindset with strong analytical skills.

 - Excellent communication and documentation abilities.

 - Bonus: Experience with UAS or defense systems.



We look forward to hearing how you can help shape the future of autonomous defense systems at Harmattan AI.

source_jobs.title: Electrical Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.



About the Role

As an Electrical Engineer, your main role will be to design and develop electronic boards and embedded systems (SoM) for UAVs (e.g., Flight Controllers, Companion Computers, Electronic Speed Controllers).

Responsibilities

 - High-performance embedded system design: Define the architecture and design the physical and electrical integration of complex systems based on processors, fast memory (DDR, Flash), and peripherals (e.g. embedded computers/calculators, sensors, video processing…).

 - Advanced schematic and routing design: Design and develop electronic schematics, and perform board placement/routing to meet design specifications, managing fast signal constraints.

 - Physical integration: Collaborate with the Mechanical Department to ensure the seamless physical integration of the boards into the final product.

 - Sensor integration: Work closely with the GNC (Guidance, Navigation, and Control) Department to ensure accurate and effective sensor integration.

 - Electromagnetic compatibility: Address and resolve EMC challenges while ensuring signal integrity.

 - Troubleshooting: Identify and resolve issues in electrical and electronic boards through effective troubleshooting.

 - Testing and Validation: Conduct testing, integration, and performance validation of electronic boards to meet operational and design requirements.

Candidate Requirements

 - Educational Background: Master’s degree in Electrical Engineering or a related field.

 - Technical Expertise:
   
   - Demonstrated experience of 5 years minimum in complex electronic system design.
   
   - In-depth knowledge of processors, memory domains (DDR, Flash), and electronic CAD tools (e.g., Altium).

 - Testing Proficiency: Proficiency in using electronic test equipment.

 - Additional Skills: Experience with Robotics or Aerospace systems is a strong advantage.

 - Problem-Solving: Ability to work autonomously and solve complex technical challenges.

 - Communication & Influence: Excellent written and verbal communication, capable of interacting with diverse principals—from junior members to senior executives.

 - Commitment: 100% dedication to Harmattan AI’s mission, vision, and ambitious growth plans, ready to go the extra mile to ensure operational excellence.



We look forward to hearing how you can help shape the future of autonomous defense systems at Harmattan AI.

source_jobs.title: RF Electronics Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.






ABOUT THE ROLE

You will join a dedicated R&D initiative at Harmattan AI focused on developing a wireless link module for drone-to-base station communications. As an RF Electronics Engineer, you will be responsible for designing and integrating PCBs and electrical systems in a compact, rugged form factor - ensuring reliability, low latency, and field readiness.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Perform the schematic design of PCBs, including component placement and routing

 - Oversee the physical integration of PCBs into the product, in collaboration with mechanical engineers

 - Address and resolve electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) issues as they arise

 - Conduct thorough testing and validation of PCBs upon reception

 - Work closely with other R&D teams to ensure seamless coordination




CANDIDATE REQUIREMENTS

 - Educational Background: Master’s degree in Electrical Engineering or a related field

 - Strong PCB design and layout experience (Altium or equivalent)

 - Experience: 2–5 years in the development of electronic boards, including testing and validation

 - Experience with RF systems and their implications on PCB design

 - Problem-Solving: Ability to work autonomously and solve complex technical challenges

 - Communication & Influence: Excellent written and verbal communication, capable of leading diverse principals – from junior members to senior executives

 - Commitment: 100% dedication to Harmattan AI’s mission, vision, and ambitious growth plans, ready to go the extra mile to ensure operational excellence





We look forward to hearing how you can help shape the future of autonomous defense systems at Harmattan AI.

source_jobs.title: GNC Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.






ABOUT THE ROLE

As a GNC Engineer on the UAV team, you’ll design and develop advanced guidance, navigation, and control (GNC) solutions. From integrating and fusing sensor data to developing control laws and flight trajectories, you'll take your work from simulation to embedded implementation—and ultimately to live flight testing.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Develop state-of-the-art navigation and sensor fusion algorithms for UAVs

 - Design and implement GNC and flight control systems

 - Build filtering and estimation strategies for robust and efficient flight performance

 - Run extensive simulations (Monte Carlo, SITL, HITL) and coverage testing

 - Analyze test flight data and refine algorithmic performance

 - Support full-stack system integration: GNSS, INS/IMU, localization, and fusion

 - Maintain and evolve a flight-proven flight computer across multiple UAV platforms




REQUIREMENTS

 - Education : Engineering school or master's degree in computer science, engineering, or a related technical field

 - 3+ years of experience in aerospace GNC systems

 - Strong foundations in control theory, estimation, and sensor integration

 - Proficient in Python and embedded C/C++

 - Experience with simulation tools (e.g., Gazebo) and version control (Git)

 - Familiarity with open-source autopilots (PX4, Ardupilot, Betaflight, etc.)

 - Hands-on electronics and integration skills: debugging, soldering, harnessing

 - Bonus: FPV pilot hobbyist





We look forward to hearing how you can help shape the future of autonomous defense systems at Harmattan AI.

source_jobs.title: Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.






ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Product Manager at Harmattan AI, you will be at the heart of company strategy, as you will be in charge of product specification and roadmap based on market and technological analysis, as well as full scale development and industrialization of the product. You will be responsible for managing timelines, resources, and cross-functional collaboration to ensure market fit and successful delivery.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Product Definition: Define product specification based on customer feedback, market analysis and technology; iterate and validate for technical, industrial and commercial feasibility.

 - Project Management: Develop, maintain, and update project timelines, ensuring all deadlines are met during the whole lifecycle of the product.

 - Technical Coordination: Oversee the architecture and the technical development of the system across R&D and industrialization phases, resolving challenges as they arise.

 - Team Leadership: Inspire and manage the project team to maintain a high level of performance and quality.

 - Cross-functional Collaboration: Act as the key interface between technical teams, the product team, and other stakeholders (especially customers, sales, marketing and program) to align efforts and ensure tenders and projects success.

 - Resource Allocation: Manage the allocation of engineering and other resources to meet project goals effectively.




CANDIDATE REQUIREMENTS

 - Master’s degree or PhD in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field, with at least 5 years of experience in technical roles.

 - Experience and ecosystem knowledge in one of the following field is highly desirable: defense systems, UAV/UxV/robotics, aerospace, radars/RF/radio, optronics, PNT, AI, or related industries.

 - Customer and product-oriented.

 - Proven experience managing cross-functional, high-complexity aerospace, defense or robotics projects that have reached industrialization.

 - Strong technical background with the ability to understand and resolve complex engineering challenges.

 - Detail-oriented with excellent documentation and organizational skills.

 - Strong communication and leadership skills, with the ability to motivate and guide teams toward success.





We look forward to hearing how you can help shape the future of autonomous defense systems at Harmattan AI.

source_jobs.title: General Application
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.



At Harmattan AI, we design and deploy advanced AI-powered systems for defense. Our work sits at the intersection of cutting-edge technology and national security, fast-moving, high-stakes, and deeply mission-driven.

We hire selectively and work with urgency, precision, and a commitment to solving problems that matter. If you're exceptional in your field and driven by building real-world impact, we want to hear from you.

Even if no listed role matches your profile, we welcome proactive applications from individuals who thrive in high-responsibility environments and bring deep expertise in areas such as:

 - Aerospace & Robotics – GNC, autonomy, flight software, mission planning

 - Embedded Systems & Hardware – FPGA, real-time systems, electronics

 - Software & AI – machine learning, perception, signal processing

 - Business, Strategy & Operations – program execution, partnerships, international expansion




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We value individuals who:

 - Set a high personal bar for quality and technical excellence

 - Take ownership and move fast under uncertainty

 - Push boundaries, challenge assumptions, and deliver under pressure

 - Are motivated by complex challenges with meaningful geopolitical stakes




WHAT TO INCLUDE

 - A resume

 - A short message telling us what you’re best at, and how you think you could contribute to our mission



We review all applications thoroughly and will reach out if there’s a potential fit.



We look forward to hearing how you can help shape the future of autonomous defense systems at Harmattan AI.

source_jobs.title: QMS/ISO Quality System Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.






ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a Quality Engineer to support the deployment and continuous improvement of our quality management system (QMS) across our international entities. Working closely with the Senior Quality Engineer, you will contribute to maintaining high-quality standards, supporting audits, and ensuring smooth communication between teams.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Quality Management System Support
   
   - Assist in the documentation and implementation of procedures and processes aligned with ISO 9001 and EN 9100 standards.
   
   - Participate in internal audits from start to finish (covering workstation, process, and supplier audits).
   
   - Contribute to the follow-up of non-conformities, corrective and preventive actions.

 - Operational Quality
   
   - Manage incoming and outgoing quality checks, including supplier and customer feedback.
   
   - Participate in the analysis of product returns and coordinate corrective actions with the technical teams.
   
   - Use quality tools such as Root Cause Analysis and Ishikawa diagrams to identify and classify potential causes of quality issues.
   
   - Support risk reduction initiatives across production and R&D teams.

 - Collaboration and Communication
   
   - Work with cross-functional teams using platforms such as Jira or Confluence to document and track quality topics.
   
   - Communicate effectively with suppliers and internal stakeholders to resolve issues and promote a strong quality culture.

 - Continuous Improvement
   
   - Participate in initiatives to improve processes, reduce risks, and increase customer satisfaction.
   
   - Collect and analyze data to support performance monitoring and reporting.




CANDIDATE REQUIREMENTS

 - Degree in quality, industrial, aeronautical, or mechanical engineering.

 - 2–3 years of relevant experience in quality engineering, preferably in the automotive, aerospace, or high-tech sectors, with at least one successful first permanent role.

 - Solid knowledge of ISO 9001/EN 9100 and ability to perform internal audits from end-to-end across different types of quality topics (process, supplier, product).

 - Experience with quality tools, including Root Cause Analysis, Ishikawa diagrams, and risk reduction methods.

 - Experience with Jira/Confluence or similar collaboration tools.

 - Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to work effectively across teams and departments in an international environment.

 - Communication & Commitment: Excellent written and verbal communication skills in French and English; full alignment with Harmattan AI’s mission and willingness to go the extra mile to support our growth.





We look forward to hearing how you can help shape the future of autonomous defense systems at Harmattan AI.

 - Support full-stack system integration: GNSS, INS/IMU, localisation, and fusion

 - Maintain and evolve a flight-proven flight computer across multiple UAV platforms


REQUIREMENTS

 - Education : Engineering school or master's degree in computer science, engineering, or a related technical field

 - 3+ years of experience in aerospace GNC systems

 - Strong foundations in control theory, estimation, and sensor integration

 - Proficient in Python and embedded C/C++

 - Experience with simulation tools (e.g., Gazebo) and version control (Git)

 - Familiarity with open-source autopilots (PX4, Ardupilot, Betaflight, etc.)

 - Hands-on electronics and integration skills: debugging, soldering, harnessing

 - Bonus: FPV pilot hobbyist



We look forward to hearing how you can help shape the future of autonomous defense systems at Harmattan AI.

source_jobs.title: Embedded Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As an Embedded Software Engineer at Harmattan AI, you will join a growing team dedicated to developing cutting-edge UAV systems. Building on the foundation established by the team, you will contribute to designing software architectures, setting up development environments, and integrating drivers and algorithms for sensors and systems. Collaborating closely with the Algorithmic teams, your expertise will help advance Harmattan AI’s mission and foster a culture of technical excellence.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Operating System: Configure and maintain the operating system (e.g., Embedded Linux or RTOS) for UAV systems.

 - Software Architecture: Participate in the improvement of the embedded software architecture, ensuring scalability and reliability.

 - Sensor Integration: Develop sensor drivers and integrate online algorithms in collaboration with the Algorithmic teams.

 - Telemetry Systems: Improve and maintain telemetry systems for the drone.

 - Coding Standards: Maintain strong coding standards and practices across the company to ensure high-quality software.

 - Tools : Collaborate in the implementation of in-house tools to accelerate software development.

 - Collaboration: Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to deliver robust, high-performance systems for field deployment.


CANDIDATE REQUIREMENTS

 - Education: Degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Robotics, or a related field.

 - Experience in Embedded Systems: Extensive experience in embedded software development, with a focus on robotics, aerospace, or related fields.

 - Programming Proficiency: Strong proficiency in C/C++; experience with Python is a plus.

 - OS Expertise: Proven track record of working with Embedded Linux or RTOS and multi-threaded/multi-process systems.

 - Sensor Development: Experience developing and integrating drivers for various sensors; ability to interpret data-sheets.

 - Debugging Skills: Hands-on experience with debugging tools (e.g., software debuggers, oscilloscopes, logic analysers).

 - Build Systems: Familiarity with build systems and version control tools (e.g., Git).

 - Firmware Knowledge: Strong understanding of firmware architecture and low-level programming.

 - Problem-Solving: Ability to work autonomously and solve complex technical challenges.

 - Communication & Influence: Excellent written and verbal communication, capable of leading diverse principals – from junior members to senior executives.

You will adapt and extend the open-source QGroundControl platform (C++/Qt) to integrate seamlessly with our proprietary hardware, adding new features and ensuring reliability, performance, and scalability across missions.

 - 3+ years of experience in software development: C++ and Qt for desktop or embedded applications.

 - Knowledge of MAVLink, PX4, or ground control systems is an advantage.

ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.






ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a skilled and forward-thinking Senior Automation Engineer to design, implement, and optimize automation systems at our production site, the model for our future global manufacturing sites.
You’ll lead automation projects that enable cost-effective, high-volume, and defense-grade production through robotics, digital integration, and lean processes.




RESPONSIBILITIES

Automation Systems

 - Design and deploy automated solutions across key manufacturing and test processes.

 - Translate manual tasks into robust automated stations (robotics, sensors, actuators, software).

 - Own equipment specifications, commissioning, and validation with suppliers and integrators.

Performance & Reliability

 - Maximize OEE through improved uptime, performance, and first-pass yield.

 - Analyze real-time data and fault logs to drive continuous improvement.

 - Support maintenance and production teams in troubleshooting and system optimization.

Digital Integration

 - Connect automation platforms to ERP/MES for live traceability and performance data.

 - Build OEE dashboards and enable predictive maintenance through data and IoT tools.

 - Contribute to digital twin and simulation projects for capacity and layout planning.

Innovation & Standardization

 - Evaluate and prototype new automation and robotics technologies.

 - Standardize automation architectures and documentation for replication in future factories.

 - Train technical staff and provide user-friendly system documentation.




CANDIDATE REQUIREMENTS

 - Degree in Automation Engineering, Mechatronics, Robotics, or equivalent.

 - 5+ years experience in manufacturing automation (design, commissioning, optimization).

 - Strong command of robotics, PLCs, sensors, motion systems, and automation safety standards (CE).

 - Interest in Industry 4.0, collaborative robotics, and machine learning applications.

 - Proven experience deploying reliable, high-availability systems.

 - Communication & Influence: Excellent written and verbal communication in French and English, capable of leading diverse principals – from junior members to senior executives.

source_jobs.title: Industrialization Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.




ABOUT THE ROLE

At Harmattan AI, we don't just design defense systems — we build them, at speed, and at scale. As an Industrialization Engineer within the Automation & Industrialization team, you will be at the heart of that challenge.

This is not a desk job. You will be on the shop floor, hands-on, turning early-stage designs into robust, repeatable production processes. From writing the first work instruction to identifying the bottleneck that no one else spotted — you own it, you fix it, you improve it.

You will work alongside engineers who push manufacturing boundaries every day, on products that have never been built before, in an environment where your ideas translate directly into action. If you are the kind of person who walks into a production line and immediately sees what could be better — this role was built for you.

Your scope spans DFM, process development, continuous improvement, and automation support — with the opportunity to grow fast in a team scaling from prototype to high-volume production.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Be present and active on the shop floor — develop work instructions, time studies, SOPs, and visual standards from direct observation and hands-on involvement

 - Identify improvement opportunities with a trained eye, act on them, and share knowledge to keep the team consistent and unblocked

 - Lead manufacturability reviews and resolve design constraints before they reach production; standardize assembly processes meeting quality, ergonomic, and safety standards

 - Drive time-and-motion analysis to set cycle time targets, track performance (cycle time, yield, rework), and deliver data-backed improvements with clear KPI targets

 - Support the transition to automated assembly and leverage robotics where applicable

 - Bridge R&D, prototype, and mass production sites — capturing and applying lessons learned across the full product line




CANDIDATE REQUIREMENTS

Essential

 - Degree in Industrial, Mechatronics Engineering, or related field

 - Comfortable working from engineering drawings, assembly schematics, and evolving work instructions

 - Familiarity with lean manufacturing principles and their application in a production or pilot environment

 - Adaptable and resourceful — at ease where instructions and designs change frequently

 - Curious, improvement-driven, and eager to take ownership quickly

 - Fluent French and English

 - Hands-on mindset; at ease on the shopfloor, not just behind a screen

 - Preferred

 - Exposure to a ramp-up, startup, or new product introduction context is a plus, not a prerequisite

 - Curiosity about automation and a desire to understand where manufacturing is heading

 - Familiarity with Odoo or any MES/ERP is a bonus

 - Comfortable operating in ambiguous environments where processes are still being built.
   
   

 - Communication & Influence: Excellent written and verbal communication in French and English, capable of leading diverse principals – from junior members to senior executives.

 - Commitment: 100% dedication to Harmattan AI’s mission, vision, and ambitious growth plans, ready to go the extra mile to ensure operational excellence.



We look forward to hearing how you can help shape the future of autonomous defense systems at Harmattan AI.

ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Drone System Validation Technician and Pilot, you are the backbone of our flight operations. You ensure that our fleet of autonomous drones is airworthy, updated, and ready for deployment at a moment's notice. You will manage the validation lab and support engineers during complex field campaigns.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Fleet Maintenance: Perform regular maintenance, repairs, and upgrades on the drone fleet (soldering, mechanical assembly, part replacement).

 - Flight Operations: Pilot drones during test campaigns and act as the safety operator.

 - Logistics: Manage inventory of spare parts, batteries, and field equipment; organize transport for test campaigns.

 - Test Execution: Execute standard test protocols (checklists) and report results to the engineering team.

 - Lab Management: Keep the workshop organised, safe, and fully stocked.


CANDIDATE REQUIREMENTS

 - Technical degree or relevant experience in electronics, mechanics, or RC modeling.

 - Excellent manual dexterity: Soldering, wiring, and mechanical assembly.

 - Organised and safety-conscious.

 - Drone Piloting: Experience piloting multi-rotor or fixed-wing UAVs (certification is a massive plus).

 - Strong interest for DIY

 - A2 English level

 - Willingness to travel for field testing.

source_jobs.title: Validation Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Software Engineer - Validation, you will build the "machine that tests the machine." You sit at the intersection of Hardware, Software, and DevOps. Your mission is to develop the hardware benches and software pipelines that allow the validation team to scale from testing 1 drone to 100 drones efficiently.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Automation: Develop Python scripts and tools to automate log analysis, telemetry parsing, and report generation.

 - CI/CD Integration: Work with the software team to integrate physical hardware tests into the continuous integration pipeline.

 - HIL Development: Design and build Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) test benches to simulate flight conditions in the lab.

 - Bonus: Design mechanical fixtures and jigs (CAD) to assist in repeatable physical testing.


CANDIDATE REQUIREMENTS

 - Background in Software Engineering, Embedded Systems, or Mechatronics.

 - Strong proficiency in Python (C++ is a plus).

 - Experience building test benches, HIL systems, or automated test equipment (ATE).

 - Familiarity with DevOps tools (Docker, Jenkins, Git) and Linux environments.

 - Bonus: A background in robotic or drones is a plus.

 - Bonus: Mechanical design skills (CAD) for building custom test rigs.

source_jobs.description:

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

About the Role

As an Automation Software Engineer, you will play a key role in designing and developing the software that powers our automated testing and inspection platforms. Working within the R&D department, you will create robust, scalable automation tools that support both product development and manufacturing operations. Your primary focus will be software development for test automation, while also collaborating on mechanical and electrical integration aspects of test systems.



Responsibilities

 - Test Automation Development – Design, implement, and maintain automated test and inspection software, primarily using Python.

 - Data Acquisition & Analysis – Build tools for real-time data collection, visualization, and analysis to improve product validation and process efficiency.

 - System Integration – Interface with sensors, cameras, motion controllers, and other hardware components to create fully automated testing workflows.

 - Cross-Team Collaboration – Partner with product development, manufacturing, and quality teams to deliver reliable and maintainable automation solutions.



Candidate Requirements

source_jobs.title: C2 Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a dedicated Software Engineer to join our C2 Core team in Paris to build the "nervous system" of our new tactical Command and Control (C2) application. You will enter a highly technical, mission-critical environment where low latency, fault tolerance, and secure data handling are paramount.

You will be responsible for the core architecture that ensures real-time telemetry, heavy video streams, and mission commands flow seamlessly between our robot fleet and internal C2 components, such as the Common Operational Picture (COP) and Mission Planner. This system is designed to run on ruggedized, resource-constrained tactical PCs in the field, often under degraded network conditions.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Robot-to-C2 Communications Gateway: Design and implement ingestion pipelines for high-frequency telemetry and video streams from multiple robots simultaneously.

 - Internal Messaging & IPC: Build the inter-process communication (IPC) or local pub/sub systems to route sensor data, user commands, and mission plans between the HMI and the robot fleet.

 - Resource Optimisation: Ensure the backend runs efficiently on local tactical PCs, managing CPU and memory footprints to prevent interference with UI or planning algorithms.

 - Fault Tolerance: Architect state machines capable of handling the chaos of tactical environments, including frequent network drops and packet loss, without corrupting the Common Operational Picture.

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with Mission Planning algorithm designers, HMI frontend developers, and UX designers to deliver a cohesive product .
   
   
   
   
   CANDIDATE REQUIREMENTS

 - Educational Background: B.S. or M.Sc http://M.Sc. in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field

 - Experience shipping production-grade applications with complex data visualization and real-time UI requirements.
   
    
   
   Technical Skills:

 - Strong command of system-level languages such as C++, Rust, or Go.

 - Deep expertise in Real-Time Networking (UDP, TCP, WebRTC, gRPC, DDS, or custom binary protocols).

 - Expertise in Systems Architecture & IPC, specifically using bounded ring buffers or zero-copy shared memory for high-concurrency pipelines.

 - Proficiency in profiling and capping resource footprints using tools like perf or cgroups.

 - Soft Skills: * Ownership & Mission-Driven: A high degree of commitment to Harmattan AI’s mission and the ability to thrive in a fast-paced startup environment.

 - Communication: Ability to mediate technical trade-offs between hardware, algorithm, and frontend teams.
   
   
   
   



We look forward to hearing how you can help shape the future of autonomous defense systems at Harmattan AI.

source_jobs.title: C2 Software Frontend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking a highly skilled Front-End Engineer to join our C2 Core team in Paris. In this role, you will be responsible for the operator's viewport into the battlefield, building the Human-Machine Interface (HMI) for our tactical Command and Control (C2) system.

You will enter a context where cognitive load management is just as critical as software performance. The UI you build will run on tactical PCs in the field, requiring seamless real-time updates for the Common Operational Picture (COP), rendering multiple live video streams, and providing an intuitive interface for planning missions and commanding a fleet of robots.

Responsibilities

 - COP Visualization: Build interactive maps and 2D/3D geospatial overlays displaying real-time robot telemetry, sensor data, and mission routes.

 - Real-Time Video Grid: Develop a low-latency, resilient video player interface capable of handling multiple simultaneous streams without freezing the main UI thread.

 - Mission Planning & Command: Create interactive tools for operators to draw waypoints, set mission parameters, and issue instant commands to the fleet.

 - Performance Architecture: Design a state management system capable of processing high-frequency data from the backend bus without causing UI jitter or memory leaks.

 - Collaborative Development: Work closely with UX/UI designers, backend engineers, and mission planning algorithm designers to refine workflows and ensure absolute minimum clicks for the operator.

 - Optimisation: Ensure the UI maintains strict performance benchmarks, such as consistent 60fps rendering, even during high-frequency map updates.
   
   


CANDIDATE REQUIREMENTS

 - Educational Background: B.S. or M.Sc http://M.Sc. in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field.

 - Professional Experience: At least 5-6 years of experience shipping production-grade web applications with complex data visualisation.
   
   
   
   Technical Expertise:

 - Deep understanding of complex state management and real-time data flow.

 - Experience with geospatial mapping libraries (e.g., Mapbox, Leaflet, Cesium) or rendering APIs (WebGL, Canvas).

 - Familiarity with modern frameworks like React or Vue.

 - Domain Knowledge: Previous experience in defense, aerospace, fintech, or high-reliability domains where real-time data rendering is critical.

 - UX & Cognitive Load Awareness: High degree of user empathy; ability to design interfaces for high-stress environments where operators cannot afford distractions or confusing error states.

 - Proven Leadership & Ownership: A "mission-driven" and "product-oriented" mindset with a willingness to take full ownership of the frontend architecture.

 - Adaptability: Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, multi-disciplinary startup environment.
   
   



We look forward to hearing how you can help shape the future of autonomous defense systems at Harmattan AI.

source_jobs.title: Mechanical Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Mechanical Engineer on Harmattan AI’s Airframe team, you will design UAV platforms and subsystems in an autonomous, fast-paced environment. You will take ownership of projects from concept through to production, ensuring optimal performance, reliability, and manufacturability.





RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design and prototype UAV platforms and complex subsystems from scratch, ensuring the right balance between durability, efficiency, and performance.

 - Take full ownership of system-level constraints, including thermal management, mass budget (weight), COGS (cost), and environmental sealing (IP ratings).

 - Work closely with R&D teams (Electrical, Software, and Industrialisation) to ensure seamless mechanical integration of sensors, payloads, and electronics.

 - Conduct rigorous testing, including stress and failure analysis. Identify risks proactively and develop the necessary tools (test benches, calculation sheets, or scripts) to validate designs for operation in extreme environments.

 - Manage your own delivery packages and technical specifications with precision, consistently meeting or exceeding requirements under tight deadlines.

 - Oversee the manufacturing process by collaborating directly with suppliers and external manufacturers, with a focus on plastic injection moulding and precision machining, to ensure quality and schedule targets are achieved.

 - Maintain the “source of truth” through Confluence and professional 2D/3D documentation. Document all designs, test results, and production workflows to support knowledge sharing and continuous improvement.




CANDIDATE REQUIREMENTS

 - Master’s degree in Mechanical Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, or a related field.

 - Proven experience designing and prototyping mechanical systems, ideally within UAVs, robotics, or defence applications.

 - Proficiency in CAD software (e.g., SolidWorks); experience with structural and thermal analysis tools (FEA) is a strong advantage.

 - Strong understanding of manufacturing processes, including high-precision machining and plastic injection moulding.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to clearly document complex technical concepts and lead cross-functional discussions.

 - Full commitment to Harmattan AI’s mission, vision, and growth plans, with a willingness to go the extra mile in pursuit of operational excellence.



We look forward to hearing how you can help shape the future of autonomous defense systems at Harmattan AI.

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer - Corporate Tooling
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.



About the Role

We are looking for a Lead Software Engineer to architect and scale our Manufacturing Execution System (MES). At Harmattan AI, we treat our MES as a core software product, not just any MES, but essential to our factory’s traceability, quality, and analytics.

As the first senior in-house hire for this module, you will transition from a high-impact Individual Contributor to an Engineering Manager, building a team. You will co-design the architecture with the Head of Corporate Tooling and set the technical standards that will allow us to scale our organization.



Responsibilities

Architecture & Strategic Ownership

 - Review and redesign the strategic architecture of the MES custom layer to support high-scale factory operations.

 - Drive technical improvements across all features, including quality management, automation, and deep integrations.

 - Establish and enforce technical standards for the broader MES footprint, covering accounting, purchasing, quality and others.



Product Development (ERP / MES)

 - Translate the current subset of ERP open-source manufacturing into a scalable, industrial-grade MES that supports our factory ramp-up.

 - Hands-on coding (modules, integrations, and code reviews).

 - Develop a version 2 of the MES for production, designed to be deployed across the factory.



Leadership & Team Building

 - Grow and manage the ERP/MES engineering team.

 - Audit existing systems and deliver a comprehensive MES roadmap within your first 90 days.

 - Mentor junior engineers and serve as the technical reference point for operational tooling.



Operational Integration

 - Work closely with stakeholders in Manufacturing, Quality, and Procurement to translate vague operational needs into clean technical specifications.

 - Collaborate directly with the Head of Manufacturing and Head of Quality to ensure the MES meets rigorous defense-tech standards.

 - As a vertically integrated industry, we consider our factory as a product.



Candidate Requirements

 - 4–7+ years of software engineering experience at a fast-paced, tech-forward company.

 - Strong architectural instincts and proficiency in Python. You have a proven track record of shipping non-trivial systems to production at scale.

 - Education: Degree from a top-tier engineering school in Software Engineering.

 - Exposure to MES or manufacturing systems is a plus. We prioritize sharpness and core engineering excellence over specific tool experience.

 - Highly ambitious, self-driven and looking for ownership.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication in English and French, capable of leading diverse principals – from junior members to senior executives ; proven ability to collaborate with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

 - Commitment: 100% dedication to Harmattan AI’s mission, vision, and ambitious growth plans, ready to go the extra mile to ensure operational excellence.



We look forward to hearing how you can help shape the future of autonomous defense systems at Harmattan AI.

As a Chief Engineer, you will own the architecture, system definition and implementation of one of our robotic systems - from concept to field deployment. You will work hand-in-hand with project managers and technical leads to translate mission needs into system-level designs, coordinate multidisciplinary engineering teams, and ensure overall system performance, robustness, and reliability.

source_jobs.title: Computer Vision Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.






ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a Computer Vision Engineer to join our Machine Learning and Computer Vision team. This role is crucial for developing core technical components across various robotics/aerospace projects.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Research & Data Preparation:
   
   - Conduct research on state-of-the-art Computer Vision methodologies. Participate in the creation and curation of training and validation datasets.
   
   - Perform statistical analyses and develop visualization tools to ensure data quality.

 - Algorithm Development & Optimization:
   
   - Build and refine training pipelines and metrics to enhance model performance.
   
   - Develop and optimize Computer Vision algorithms for multiple robotics/aerospace projects.

 - Deployment & Integration: Implement ML/CV models into production-ready environments. Ensure seamless integration with Harmattan AI’s systems and conduct rigorous code reviews.

 - Validation & Monitoring: Test algorithms in real-world environments and develop monitoring tools. Track model performance and continuously improve deployed solutions.

 - Cross-Team Collaboration: Work closely with software and simulation teams to align development with system requirements. Communicate findings effectively to stakeholders.




CANDIDATE REQUIREMENTS

 - Educational Background: A degree from a top-tier engineering school or university (Master’s degree in Computer Science or related field, PhD is a plus)

 - Technical Expertise: Strong mathematical foundations, coding skills (Python, C++ is a plus) and hands-on ML/CV project experience. Experience in top AI companies is a huge plus.

 - Passion for ML: Enthusiasm for Machine Learning and Computer Vision.

 - Strong Communication & Teamwork: Ability to collaborate effectively with diverse teams

 - Commitment: 100% dedication to Harmattan AI’s mission, vision, and ambitious growth plans, ready to go the extra mile to ensure operational excellence.





We look forward to hearing how you can help shape the future of autonomous defense systems at Harmattan AI.

source_jobs.title: US Manufacturing VP
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As Manufacturing VP USA, you will lead the establishment and scaling of Harmattan AI’s manufacturing capabilities in the United States. This role is critical to enabling the company’s rapid expansion and supporting the delivery of advanced defense technologies to U.S. customers.

You will be responsible for building and managing Harmattan AI’s U.S. manufacturing ecosystem, including the setup and optimization of production capabilities through industrial partners and internal operations as the company scales.

You will ensure alignment with Harmattan AI’s global industrial strategy, quality standards, and operational best practices.

This role requires a hands-on leader capable of structuring and scaling reliable industrial capacity in the United States, while coordinating closely with engineering, supply chain, and operations teams across multiple geographies.

You will work in close coordination with the Head of Manufacturing & Supply Chain and jointly develop and deploy the U.S. manufacturing strategy, ensuring strong alignment with Harmattan AI’s global industrial roadmap while maintaining clear execution ownership in the U.S.




RESPONSIBILITIES




U.S. MANUFACTURING STRATEGY & EXECUTION

 - Develop and execute Harmattan AI’s U.S. manufacturing strategy in close alignment with the Head of Manufacturing & SCM, establishing a scalable and resilient industrial footprint to support rapid growth.

 - Structure and manage manufacturing partnerships and production capabilities in the United States, ensuring reliable, high-quality output.

 - Contribute to the progressive development of Harmattan AI’s long-term industrial capabilities in the U.S. as the company scales.

 - Ensure alignment between U.S. manufacturing execution and Harmattan AI’s global industrial strategy.


PRODUCTION & SCALE MANAGEMENT

 - Direct the setup and operation of production capabilities in the United States, ensuring consistent quality standards and on-time delivery.

 - Oversee manufacturing execution and optimize production processes to meet scaling demands in dynamic, high-pressure environments.

 - Monitor and improve key manufacturing performance indicators such as throughput, yield, production cost, and quality.

 - Ensure the successful transition from engineering prototypes to scalable industrial production.


SUPPLY CHAIN MANAGEMENT

 - Develop and maintain relationships with U.S.-based manufacturing partners, suppliers, and industrial stakeholders.

 - Coordinate with global supply chain teams to ensure alignment with Harmattan AI’s procurement and sourcing strategies.

 - Manage production planning, logistics, and inventory flows to ensure reliable product delivery.

 - Contribute to building a resilient and scalable U.S. industrial ecosystem supporting production activities.


PROCESS IMPROVEMENT

 - Identify opportunities to streamline workflows and boost operational efficiency by implementing best practices in Lean manufacturing, Six Sigma, or equivalent methodologies.

 - Drive continuous improvement initiatives to reduce waste, increase throughput, and enhance overall product quality.

 - Implement structured processes to improve manufacturing reliability and operational performance.


COLLABORATION & CROSS-FUNCTIONAL COORDINATION

 - Serve as a key liaison between Engineering, Operations, Supply Chain, and Finance for manufacturing-related activities in

source_jobs.title: Chief Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.





About the Role

As a Chief Engineer, you will own the architecture, system definition and implementation of one of our robotic systems — from concept to field deployment. You will work hand-in-hand with project managers and technical leads to translate mission needs into system-level designs, coordinate multidisciplinary engineering teams, and ensure overall system performance, robustness, and reliability.



Responsibilities

 - System Architecture: Transform system requirements into architectures solutions.

 - Technical Coordination: Lead cross-functional design across mechanical, electrical, software, GNC, and ML teams.

 - Trade-Off & Design Analysis: Conduct feasibility studies, trade-off analyses, and system-level simulations to guide technical choices.

 - Integration & Validation: Oversee system integration, prototyping, and validation, ensuring performance targets are achieved.

 - System Reviews: Drive design and architecture reviews, maintaining consistency between requirements, design, and testing.

 - Continuous Improvement: Implement processes to enhance requirements traceability, system documentation, and validation workflows.



Candidate Requirements

 - 10+ years of experience in system engineering within robotics, aerospace, or defense sectors.

 - MSc in Engineering (any field); PhD is a plus.

 - Proven experience leading full system lifecycles (concept → prototype → validation).

 - Strong background in system architecture, requirements engineering, and trade-off analysis.

 - Broad engineering culture with understanding of mechanical, electrical, and embedded systems.

 - Experience coordinating multi-disciplinary teams and managing technical interfaces.

 - Rigorous, structured, and mission-driven mindset with strong analytical skills.

 - Excellent communication and documentation abilities.

 - Bonus: Experience with UAS or defense systems.





We look forward to hearing how you can help shape the future of autonomous defense systems at Harmattan AI.

source_jobs.title: Computer Vision Engineer (VIO)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Harmattan AI is a next-generation defense prime building autonomous and scalable defense systems. Following the close of a $200M Series B, valuing the company at $1.4 billion, we are expanding our teams and capabilities to deliver mission-critical systems to allied forces.

Our work is guided by clear values: building technologies with real-world impact, pursuing excellence in everything we do, setting ambitious goals, and taking on the hardest technical challenges. We operate in a demanding environment where rigor, ownership, and execution are expected.






ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a Computer Vision Engineer to contribute to the development of the front-end of our visual inertial odometry (VIO) algorithmic stack.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - VIO Front-end Algorithm development:
   
   - Matching between frames and stereo pairs
   
   - Calibration of cameras intrinsic and extrinsic parameters
   
   - Detection of obstruction

 - Implementation and optimization of the algorithmic stack for our embedded platforms

 - Testing, Validation & Monitoring: algorithms testing in simulation and real-world environments, development of inspection and monitoring tools..

 - Cross-Team Collaboration: Work closely with system engineers, optical engineers, software engineers. Communicate findings effectively to stakeholders.




CANDIDATE REQUIREMENTS

 - Educational Background: Master’s degree in Robotics, Physics, Computer Science or related field, PhD is a plus

 - Technical Expertise: Strong mathematical foundations, coding skills in both Python and C++

 - Hands-on VIO project experience. Experience with VIO on industrial products is a huge plus.

 - Passion for Computer Vision and Robotics

 - Strong Communication & Teamwork: Ability to collaborate effectively with diverse teams

 - Commitment: 100% dedication to Harmattan’s mission, vision, and ambitious growth plans, ready to go the extra mile to ensure operational excellence.





We look forward to hearing how you can help shape the future of autonomous defense systems at Harmattan AI.

notes: Defense prime with autonomous and scalable defense systems
